title: Object-Format Transition, Compatibility, and Disaster Defense · Graph Lens: Gita Git3 Breaking Readiness
summary: An organization must prepare for Git 3.0 defaults and SHA-256 repositories while recovering a damaged mirror, validating tooling, and preserving collaboration across object formats. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita git3 breaking readiness increment in Git 3.0 readiness and disaster-recovery defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inventory scripts, parsers, databases, APIs, hooks, CI, hosting, and documentation for Git 3.0 breaking defaults, long IDs, and version-gated behavior.
Evaluate compatibility object formats, translation tables, protocol exchange, clone and fetch interoperability, tooling assumptions, and repository immutability limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Git3 Breaking Readiness
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G74 is a theory in a disposable repository. the repository stores SHA-256 objects while one integration still assumes 40-character IDs. The learner must predict format evidence separates storage identity from accepted input and displayed output formats, run git rev-parse --show-object-format=storage,input,output, explain storage reports sha256 and the audit parser fails its fixed-width object-ID contract, test this changed condition: restore a bundle into a fresh same-format repository and reject an incompatible toolchain, and preserve this recovery boundary: preserve trusted refs, object stores, translation data, manifests, and exact loss accounting.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Gita Git3 Breaking Readiness contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inventory scripts, parsers, databases, apis, hooks, ci, hosting, and documentation for git 3.0 breaking defaults, long ids, and version-gated behavior.

content.code:
# Worked focus: gita-git3-breaking-readiness — Inventory scripts, parsers, databases, APIs, hooks, CI, hosting, and documentation for Git 3.0 breaking defaults, long IDs, and version-gated behavior.
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 2
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: the repository stores SHA-256 objects while one integration still assumes 40-character IDs
git rev-parse --show-object-format=storage,input,output
# expected: format evidence separates storage identity from accepted input and displayed output formats
# observation: storage reports sha256 and the audit parser fails its fixed-width object-ID contract
# changed-case: restore a bundle into a fresh same-format repository and reject an incompatible toolchain
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: preserve trusted refs, object stores, translation data, manifests, and exact loss accounting
# transfer-limit: object-format migration and Git 3.0 rollout require current hosting and tool compatibility trials
